title: Translations. - Lyrisches Intermezzo. Xlv. (From Heine.)
content:
In the sunny summer morning
     Into the garden I come;
    The flowers are whispering and talking,
     But for me, I wander dumb.

    The flowers are whispering and talking;
     They pity my look so wan:
    "Thou must not be cross with our sister,
     Thou sorrowful, pale-faced man!"
meaning:
"Translations. - Lyrisches Intermezzo. Xlv. (From Heine.)" is a quintessential example of George MacDonald's signature style...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
